metric_name: Study price
Report page count
metric_value: 106 pages

metric_name: Report publication date
metric_value: December 1998

metric_name: Primary research method
metric_value: Combined phone, fax, and Internet survey administered autumn 1998
notes: Combined primary and secondary sources methodology

metric_name: Internet adoption status in commercial IT
metric_value: Emerging — Aberdeen Internet Barometer introduced to track adoption
notes: Internet adoption across verticals was uneven; financial services and communications led

metric_name: Mainframe usage status
metric_value: Declining but persistent; tracked via Aberdeen Mainframe Barometer
notes: Mainframe usage highest in financial services and large enterprises

metric_name: IT outsourcing trend
metric_value: Growing; tracked via Aberdeen Outsourcing Barometer

metric_name: IT spending as % of revenue — financial services
metric_value: Highest of all verticals
notes: Financial services consistently spends highest IT/revenue ratio; typically 7-10% in late 1990s

metric_name: IT spending as % of revenue — health and social services
metric_value: Moderate; significant growth potential identified

metric_name: IT spending as % of revenue — retail and hospitality
metric_value: Below average relative to other verticals
notes: Retail historically underspends on IT relative to revenue

metric_name: IT spending type breakdown — discrete manufacturing
metric_value: Analyzed by internal vs. external and hardware vs. software/services

metric_name: IT spending type breakdown — process manufacturing
metric_value: Analyzed by internal vs. external and hardware vs. software/services

metric_name: IT spending as % of revenue — communications and information
metric_value: High; sector is itself an IT producer driving above-average internal IT investment

metric_name: IT spending profile — energy and utilities
metric_value: Moderate; Y2K remediation driving above-average 1998-1999 spending
notes: Y2K was major IT budget driver in energy/utilities in 1998-1999

metric_name: IT spending as % of revenue — business services
metric_value: Above average; professional services firms are intensive IT users

metric_name: IT spending profile — wholesale trade
metric_value: Analyzed; lower than financial services but growing with e-commerce adoption

metric_name: IT spending profile — transportation and travel
metric_value: High relative to revenue; travel industry was early internet adopter
notes: Airlines' GDS systems made transport sector an early IT leader

metric_name: Software and services vs. hardware share of IT budget
metric_value: Software and services growing as share; hardware share declining
notes: Secular shift from hardware to software began in early 1990s; confirmed by 1998 data

metric_name: Internet adoption trajectory across verticals
metric_value: Internet will prove most valuable in B2B supply chain rather than consumer retail
notes: Aberdeen predicted B2B Internet would outperform B2C; consistent with e-procurement thesis

metric_name: Internet adoption outcome across verticals
metric_value: Universal commercial Internet adoption achieved by 2005; B2B e-commerce exceeded $1T by 2002
notes: Forrester Research 1999 projected B2B e-commerce at $43B in 1998 vs. $8B consumer; B2B prediction validated

metric_name: IT outsourcing growth trend
metric_value: IT outsourcing will continue to grow across all verticals

metric_name: IT outsourcing growth — actual outcome
metric_value: Global IT outsourcing market exceeded $300B annually by 2019; grew in every major vertical
notes: Gartner and Statista data confirm massive outsourcing growth from 1998 baseline

metric_name: IT spending breakdown framework — hardware vs. software/services
metric_value: Internal vs. external x hardware vs. software/services 2x2 matrix applied per vertical
notes: Aberdeen's spending matrix became standard analyst framework for IT budget benchmarking

metric_name: Enterprise size effect on IT spending
metric_value: IT spending heavily skewed toward larger enterprises in all verticals
notes: Small businesses severely underrepresented in IT investment despite making up most enterprises by count

metric_name: Aberdeen barometer framework
metric_value: Three barometers: Internet adoption; mainframe usage; outsourcing adoption — novel proprietary metrics
notes: Aberdeen's barometer concept was methodological innovation in IT market research

metric_name: Financial services IT leadership
metric_value: Financial services sector leads all verticals in IT spending intensity and adoption of new technologies
notes: Consistent with industry knowledge; banking/insurance have always been leading IT adopters

metric_name: Short-term IT spending growth potential by vertical
metric_value: Identified verticals with highest near-term (1999-2000) IT spending growth potential
notes: Y2K and ERP adoption were key drivers of near-term growth predictions

metric_name: New technology deployment tracking
metric_value: Aberdeen barometer tracks deployment of new technologies across verticals
notes: ERP, groupware, internet applications were 'new technologies' being tracked
